target.title: chore: fold WebChat auto TTS proof into QA
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a QA maturity accounting gap where the existing WebChat auto-TTS proof lived as a standalone repro script instead of durable QA Lab evidence for existing media coverage IDs.

## Why This Change Was Made

This folds `scripts/repro/webchat-auto-tts-live-proof.mjs` into a QA-owned Vitest scenario and deletes the standalone repro path. The scenario claims primary coverage only for existing IDs it directly proves: `media.tts` and `media.outbound-voice-audio-delivery`; it intentionally does not claim `gateway.webchat-hosting` because this proof does not exercise a hosted WebChat browser surface.

## User Impact

Developers and release operators can now count the WebChat auto-TTS proof through `qa suite` / `qa coverage` instead of rediscovering or manually running an unlinked repro script.

## Evidence

Primary coverage claims:

- `media.tts`: the QA test enables `messages.tts`, installs a mock speech provider, verifies WebChat block chunks do not synthesize intermediate audio, and verifies the final WebChat tail performs exactly one TTS synthesis with spoken text and trusted local media metadata.
- `media.outbound-voice-audio-delivery`: the same final TTS payload is passed through WebChat audio block construction, proving trusted local TTS audio becomes an audio attachment while an untrusted local path is rejected.

Validation run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/e2e/qa-lab/media/webchat-auto-tts.e2e.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode mock-openai --scenario webchat-auto-tts --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/webchat-auto-tts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig test/tsconfig.json test/e2e/qa-lab/media/webchat-auto-tts.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.json --pretty false`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

One attempted type probe using `test/tsconfig.json` failed because that project sets `rootDir` to `test` and rejects existing source imports; the shared test project above is the relevant passing typecheck.
target.author: romneyda
